reasoning_model_1: Model 1 successfully synthesizes the target text 'Every morning I wake up on time and start a new day' between 00:00 and 00:04. During this segment, the rhythm is reasonably steady and clear. However, from 00:05 to 00:13, the model hallucinates a significant amount of unrequested text ('Steady and sure, like the tide coming in...'). This is a major content accuracy violation. While the rhythm of the added text is stylistically steady and poetic, the model failed to adhere to the script constraint. According to the evaluation criteria, the presence of such significant hallucinations constitutes a major issue, rendering the audio unusable for the specific command despite the paralinguistic attempt.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 generates the audio strictly adhering to the provided script 'Every morning, I wake up on time and start a new day' (00:00-00:03). It contains no hallucinations, omissions, or mispronunciations. The delivery follows a steady, even-paced rhythm as requested, with appropriate spacing between phrases ('Every morning' [pause] 'I wake up on time' [pause] 'and start a new day'). The cadence is stable and consistent, effectively conveying the 'steady rhythm' feature without compromising content accuracy or naturalness.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 commits a critical error in Content Accuracy by hallucinating a continuation of the script (a poem about steadiness) which was not requested. While Model 1's rhythm in the hallucinated section was indeed steady, the failure to stop after the target sentence violates the primary instruction of reading the specific sentence. Model 2 followed the instructions perfectly, delivering the correct text with the requested steady rhythm and high naturalness.
